015 */
016package org.consensusj.jsonrpc;
017
018import org.jspecify.annotations.Nullable;
019import org.slf4j.Logger;
020import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;
021
022import javax.net.ssl.SSLContext;
023import javax.net.ssl.TrustManager;
024import javax.net.ssl.TrustManagerFactory;
025import javax.net.ssl.X509TrustManager;
026import java.io.FileInputStream;
027import java.io.FileNotFoundException;
028import java.io.IOException;
029import java.io.InputStream;
030import java.nio.file.Path;
031import java.security.KeyManagementException;
032import java.security.KeyStore;
033import java.security.KeyStoreException;
034import java.security.NoSuchAlgorithmException;
035import java.security.cert.CertificateException;
036import java.security.cert.X509Certificate;
037import java.util.ArrayList;
038import java.util.Arrays;
039import java.util.List;
040
041/**
042 * A Trust Manager that adds a second trust store (key store) to the default trust store.
043 * See: <a href="https://stackoverflow.com/questions/48790981/load-java-trust-store-at-runtime-after-jvm-have-been-launched/58695061#58695061">...</a>
044 */
045public class CompositeTrustManager implements X509TrustManager {
046    private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(CompositeTrustManager.class);
047    private static final char[] password = new char[]{'c','h','a','n','g','e','i','t'};
048
049    private final List<X509TrustManager> trustManagerList;
050
051    /**
052     * @param trustStoreStream Input stream for Java keystore with additional certificates
053     */
054    public CompositeTrustManager(InputStream trustStoreStream) {
055        try (trustStoreStream) {
056            try {
057                trustManagerList = List.of(
058                    getCustomTrustManager(trustStoreStream),
059                    getDefaultTrustManager()
060                );
061            } catch (CertificateException | KeyStoreException | NoSuchAlgorithmException e) {
062                log.error("Exception: ", e);
063                throw new RuntimeException(e);
064            }
065        } catch (IOException e) {
066            log.error("Exception: ", e);
067            throw new RuntimeException(e);
068        }
069    }
070
071    /**
072     * @param trustStorePath Path to a Java keystore with additional certificates
073     * @throws FileNotFoundException If the file doesn't exist
074     */
075    public CompositeTrustManager(Path trustStorePath) throws FileNotFoundException {
076        this(new FileInputStream(trustStorePath.toFile()));
077    }
078
079    /**
080     * Used to create an SSLContext using {@link CompositeTrustManager}
081     * See: <a href="https://stackoverflow.com/questions/859111/how-can-i-use-different-certificates-on-specific-connections">...</a>
082     */
083    public static SSLContext getCompositeSSLContext(Path trustStorePath) throws NoSuchAlgorithmException, KeyManagementException, FileNotFoundException {
084        TrustManager tm = new CompositeTrustManager(trustStorePath);
085        return getSSLContext(tm);
086    }
087
088
089    /**
090     * Used to create an SSLContext using {@link CompositeTrustManager}
091     * See: <a href="https://stackoverflow.com/questions/859111/how-can-i-use-different-certificates-on-specific-connections">...</a>
092     */
093    public static SSLContext getCompositeSSLContext(InputStream trustStoreStream) throws NoSuchAlgorithmException, KeyManagementException {
094        TrustManager tm = new CompositeTrustManager(trustStoreStream);
095        return getSSLContext(tm);
096    }
097
098    /**
099     * Create an SSLContext using an alternative Trust Store
100     */
101    public static SSLContext getAlternateSSLContext(Path trustStorePath) throws KeyStoreException, IOException, NoSuchAlgorithmException, KeyManagementException, CertificateException {
102        return getAlternateSSLContext(new FileInputStream(trustStorePath.toFile()));
103    }
104
105    /**
106     * Create an SSLContext using an alternative Trust Store
107     * @param trustStoreStream inputStream with Trust Store (password must be 'changeit')
108     */
109    public static SSLContext getAlternateSSLContext(InputStream trustStoreStream) throws KeyStoreException, IOException, NoSuchAlgorithmException, KeyManagementException, CertificateException {
110        X509TrustManager trustManager = getCustomTrustManager(trustStoreStream);
111        return getSSLContext( trustManager );
112    }
113
114    private static SSLContext getSSLContext(TrustManager trustManager) throws NoSuchAlgorithmException, KeyManagementException {
115        SSLContext ctx = SSLContext.getInstance("TLS");
116        ctx.init(null, new TrustManager[] {trustManager}, null);
117        return ctx;
118    }
119
120    @Override
121    public void checkClientTrusted(X509Certificate[] chain, String authType) throws CertificateException {
122        for (X509TrustManager trustManager : trustManagerList) {
123            try {
124                trustManager.checkClientTrusted(chain, authType);
125                return;
126            } catch (CertificateException e) {
127                // maybe the next trust manager will trust it, don't break the loop
128            }
129        }
130        throw new CertificateException("None of the TrustManagers trust this certificate chain");
131    }
132
133    @Override
134    public void checkServerTrusted(X509Certificate[] chain, String authType) throws CertificateException {
135        for (X509TrustManager trustManager : trustManagerList) {
136            try {
137                trustManager.checkServerTrusted(chain, authType);
138                return;
139            } catch (CertificateException e) {
140                // maybe the next trust manager will trust it, don't break the loop
141            }
142        }
143        throw new CertificateException("None of the TrustManagers trust this certificate chain");
144    }
145
146    @Override
147    public X509Certificate[] getAcceptedIssuers() {
148        List<X509Certificate> certs = new ArrayList<>();
149        for (X509TrustManager trustManager : trustManagerList) {
150            certs.addAll(Arrays.asList(trustManager.getAcceptedIssuers()));
151        }
152        return certs.toArray(new X509Certificate[0]);
153    }
154
155    private static X509TrustManager getCustomTrustManager(InputStream trustStream) throws CertificateException, KeyStoreException, NoSuchAlgorithmException, IOException {
156        return createTrustManager(trustStream);
157    }
158
159    private static X509TrustManager getDefaultTrustManager() throws CertificateException, KeyStoreException, NoSuchAlgorithmException, IOException {
160        return createTrustManager(null);
161    }
162
163    private static X509TrustManager createTrustManager(@Nullable InputStream trustStream) throws KeyStoreException, NoSuchAlgorithmException, CertificateException, IOException {
164        // Now get trustStore
165        KeyStore trustStore = KeyStore.getInstance(KeyStore.getDefaultType());
166
167        // load the stream to your store
168        trustStore.load(trustStream, password);
169
170        // initialize a trust manager factory with the trusted store
171        TrustManagerFactory trustFactory = TrustManagerFactory.getInstance(TrustManagerFactory.getDefaultAlgorithm());
172        trustFactory.init(trustStore);
173
174        // get the trust managers from the factory
175        TrustManager[] trustManagers = trustFactory.getTrustManagers();
176        for (TrustManager trustManager : trustManagers) {
177            if (trustManager instanceof X509TrustManager) {
178                return (X509TrustManager) trustManager;
179            }
180        }
181        throw new CertificateException("No X509TrustManager available");
182    }
183}
